utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 sections. Now check if each is fully specified with details and costs: 1) health_coverage: includes physical and mental health insurance, named plans, monthly costs, policy numbers, coverage features, and a total health cost. Fully specified. 2) property_coverage: home/contents insurance with a named policy, monthly cost, policy number, coverage limits, and a total property cost. Fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: named policy, monthly cost, policy number, coverage features, and total travel cost. Fully specified. 4) additional_services: includes a specific enhancement service (professional liability), cost, policy number, coverage limit, and total additional cost. Fully specified. 5) total_budget: monthly and annual cost breakdown, savings vs current, and a cost breakdown. Fully specified. Therefore, all 5/5 items are included and fully detailed.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I identified each policy name in the final coverage plan and matched it exactly to the corresponding option in the ground-truth ratings. The matches are: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health) -> rating 9 under primary_health_coverage.physical_health; LifeFirst Standard Plan (mental health) -> rating 9 under primary_health_coverage.mental_health; VitalPlus Standard Coverage (property) -> rating 7 under property_protection.home_content; PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel) -> rating 6 under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance; Marketing Professional Liability is not listed anywhere in the ground-truth ratings, so it cannot be mapped.
